config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that opens the door to abuse by the majority or the state. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting people’s dignity, freedom, and safety first, then balancing those rights with the common good when there is a real and serious need. From my perspective, collective interests are important, but they should not automatically override individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan oleh mayoritas atau pemerintah. Hak individu bukan sekadar hambatan bagi masyarakat; justru itu fondasi agar masyarakat tetap adil, bebas, dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Saya paham argumen bahwa kadang-kadang kepentingan bersama perlu dibatasi demi keselamatan publik, tetapi pengecualian seperti itu harus sempit, jelas, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu bisa dikorbankan terlalu mudah, maka "kepentingan masyarakat" berubah jadi alasan kosong untuk menekan orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because in practice that often becomes a license for the majority or the state to silence weaker people. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to "the common good" can be used to pressure conformity, so I am cautious about any claim that collective interests come first by default. Society does matter, but it is strongest when it protects dignity, freedom, and fairness for each person, not when it treats individual rights as disposable. I would only accept limiting individual rights when the harm is real, specific, and tightly controlled, not as a broad principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena bagi saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami cukup jelas menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi masyarakat” dipakai terlalu longgar, itu sering jadi pembenaran untuk mayoritas atau pemerintah menekan orang yang lebih lemah. Hak individu bukan musuh kepentingan publik; justru hak itulah yang membuat masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Saya hanya mau menerima pembatasan hak dalam keadaan yang benar-benar mendesak, spesifik, dan bisa diawasi ketat, bukan sebagai aturan default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a default principle. In Indonesia, I have seen how the language of "common good" can sometimes be used to pressure people into silence or conformity, so I am very wary of giving society automatic authority over the individual. A fair society is not one that treats rights as disposable; it is one that protects dignity and freedom while limiting rights only in truly serious, narrowly defined cases. If collective interests always come first, the strongest group usually wins and the weakest person pays the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, argumen “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan mayoritas, birokrasi, atau pemerintah menekan orang yang paling rentan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang mencegah masyarakat berubah jadi sewenang-wenang. Saya hanya bisa menerima pembatasan hak jika ancamannya nyata, sempit, dan diawasi ketat, bukan sebagai alasan default yang kabur.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
